questions.question: How many players are on a standard soccer team on the field?
questions.explanation: Each team fields 11 players including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'slam dunk'?
questions.explanation: A slam dunk scores by pushing the ball through the hoop.

questions.question: What country hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics?
questions.explanation: The 2016 Olympics were held in Rio de Janeiro.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most Grand Slam titles in men's tennis?
questions.explanation: Djokovic has won 24 Grand Slam singles titles.

questions.question: In cricket, how many runs is a 'century'?
questions.explanation: Scoring 100+ runs in a single innings is a century.

questions.question: What is the diameter of a basketball hoop in inches?
questions.explanation: The official NBA rim diameter is 18 inches.

questions.question: In which year were women first allowed to compete in the Olympics?
questions.explanation: Women first competed at the 1900 Paris Olympics.

questions.question: What is the only country to have played in every FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: Brazil has participated in all FIFA World Cup tournaments since 1930.

questions.question: What martial art is an Olympic sport that originated in Korea?
questions.explanation: Taekwondo became an official Olympic sport in 2000.

questions.question: What sport is played at Wimbledon?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament, first held in 1877.

questions.question: How many periods are in a standard ice hockey game?
questions.explanation: Each period is 20 minutes long.

questions.question: What is the national sport of Japan?
questions.explanation: Sumo has been practiced in Japan for centuries.

questions.question: In which sport do you use a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: A shuttlecock (birdie) is hit back and forth over a net.

questions.question: How many points is a touchdown worth in American football?
questions.explanation: A touchdown is worth 6 points, with a possible extra point or 2-point conversion.

questions.question: What color is the middle ring of the Olympic logo?
questions.explanation: The five rings from left to right are blue, yellow, black, green, and red.

questions.question: What sport uses a puck?
questions.explanation: A hockey puck is a hard rubber disk.

questions.question: Who has won the most Olympic gold medals?
questions.explanation: Phelps won 23 Olympic gold medals in swimming.

questions.question: What is the Tour de France?
questions.correctAnswer: An annual multi-stage bicycle race in France
questions.explanation: The Tour de France is the world's most prestigious cycling race.

questions.question: How long is a marathon?
questions.explanation: The distance commemorates Pheidippides' run from Marathon to Athens.

questions.question: What country invented baseball?
questions.explanation: Baseball evolved from older bat-and-ball games in America in the 1800s.

questions.question: How many players are on a volleyball team on the court?
questions.explanation: Each team has 6 players who rotate positions.

questions.question: What is a 'hat trick' in soccer?
questions.correctAnswer: Scoring three goals in one game
questions.explanation: The term originated in cricket and spread to other sports.

questions.question: What is the highest possible score in a single frame of bowling?
questions.explanation: A strike in the 10th frame can score up to 30 with bonus balls.

questions.question: What country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won 5 World Cup titles.

questions.question: In golf, what is a score of one under par called?
questions.explanation: A birdie is one stroke under par for a hole.

questions.question: What is the oldest continuously held sporting event?
questions.explanation: The America's Cup began in 1851.

questions.question: What does the 'F1' in Formula 1 stand for?
questions.explanation: The 'formula' refers to the set of rules all cars must follow.

questions.question: In which year did the first modern Olympics take place?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympics were held in Athens, Greece.

questions.question: What is the Haka?
questions.correctAnswer: A traditional Māori war dance performed by New Zealand rugby teams
questions.explanation: The All Blacks perform the Haka before international rugby matches.

questions.question: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal?
questions.explanation: Maradona scored the controversial goal against England in the 1986 World Cup.

questions.question: What sport does Tiger Woods play?
questions.explanation: Tiger Woods is one of the greatest golfers of all time.

questions.question: How many bases are there in baseball?
questions.explanation: The bases are first, second, third, and home plate.

questions.question: What color card means a player is sent off in soccer?
questions.explanation: A red card results in immediate ejection from the game.

questions.question: What is the height of a regulation basketball hoop?
questions.explanation: The standard height has been 10 feet since basketball's invention.

questions.question: What sport is played in the Super Bowl?
questions.explanation: The Super Bowl is the NFL championship game.

questions.question: What is the fastest ball sport in the world?
questions.explanation: Jai alai balls (pelotas) have been clocked at over 300 km/h.

questions.question: What is a perfect score in gymnastics?
questions.explanation: Nadia Comăneci scored the first perfect 10 at the 1976 Olympics.

questions.question: In what sport is the Davis Cup contested?
questions.explanation: The Davis Cup is the premier international team event in men's tennis.

questions.question: What sport does Serena Williams play?
questions.explanation: Serena Williams has won 23 Grand Slam singles titles.

questions.question: How many innings are in a standard baseball game?
questions.explanation: Each team bats in each inning.

questions.question: What is an 'ace' in tennis?
questions.correctAnswer: A serve the opponent cannot return
questions.explanation: An ace results in a point won on the serve alone.

questions.question: What boxing weight class is the heaviest?
questions.explanation: Heavyweight is typically for boxers over 200 lbs.

questions.question: What is the Ryder Cup?
questions.correctAnswer: A biennial golf competition between Europe and the USA
questions.explanation: The Ryder Cup has been played since 1927.

questions.question: What is the fastest 100m sprint time recorded?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt set the record at the 2009 World Championships.

questions.question: What country is known for inventing rugby?
questions.explanation: Rugby is said to have originated at Rugby School in 1823.

questions.question: How many rings are on the Olympic flag?
questions.explanation: The five rings represent the five inhabited continents.

questions.question: What is 'LBW' in cricket?
questions.explanation: LBW is a dismissal when the ball would have hit the wicket.

questions.question: Who has won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
questions.explanation: Messi has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards.

questions.question: What is the object hit in ice hockey called?
questions.explanation: A standard puck weighs about 170 grams.

questions.question: What distance is a triathlon's swimming portion in an Ironman?
questions.explanation: An Ironman triathlon also includes a 180 km bike ride and a full marathon.
